# Shell Over Edge

> Reach any shell from anywhere. Production base URL: `https://soe.stoff.dev`.

Shell Over Edge creates short-lived 8-character session codes. A bootstrap starts a generated relay agent immediately and can warm a native agent download in the background. The agent long-polls the Worker for commands, executes them locally, then posts plain text output back.

## Public API

- Create POSIX bootstrap: `GET /a`
- Create PowerShell bootstrap: `GET /a.ps1`
- Create POSIX session: `POST /api/sessions`
- Create PowerShell session: `POST /api/sessions.ps1`
- Send command: `POST /api/sessions/<code>/send`
- End session: `POST /api/sessions/<code>/end`

## Direct Internals

- Get ICE config: `GET /api/sessions/<code>/ice`
- Publish direct signal: `POST /api/sessions/<code>/signals`
- List direct signals: `GET /api/sessions/<code>/signals?role=agent`

## Quick Use

```sh
curl -sS https://soe.stoff.dev/a | sh
curl -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/api/sessions/<code>/send --data 'pwd'
curl -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/api/sessions/<code>/end
```

```powershell
irm https://soe.stoff.dev/a.ps1 | iex
curl.exe -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/api/sessions/<code>/send --data "pwd"
curl.exe -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/api/sessions/<code>/end
```

## Command Payloads

Default `/send` body is raw text and returns raw command output.

Use JSON only when setting options:

```json
{"body":"pwd","cwd":"/tmp","timeoutSeconds":30}
```

`timeout` is also accepted. Timeouts are clamped to 1-3600 seconds. The synchronous `/send` waits up to the platform-safe bridge wait and returns `504` if no result arrives.

## Direct Upgrade

The relay route is the universal fallback. Rich helpers can use the public service as rendezvous: fetch ICE config when using WebRTC, exchange short-lived direct signals, try a direct stream with a small timeout, then fall back to `/send` if direct fails.

HTTP direct signal payload:

```json
{"role":"agent","transport":"http","url":"http://127.0.0.1:9000/command","priority":1,"ttlSeconds":60}
```

WebRTC offer signal payload:

```json
{"role":"client","transport":"webrtc","data":{"type":"offer","sdp":"v=0\r\n"},"priority":1,"ttlSeconds":60}
```

Direct command endpoints should accept the same JSON command payload and return plain command output with `X-Exit-Code` when available.

## Invariants

- Sessions are short code capabilities.
- The response header `X-Session-Id` contains the code for newly created sessions.
- Generated agents print the code and copy it to the clipboard when possible.
- Most responses are plain text by design.
- No bearer tokens, helper tokens, agent tokens, URL tokens, or auth headers are used in the current API.
- Generated curl-first agents do not open inbound direct listeners by default.
- Retired endpoints such as `/commands`, `/events`, `/upload`, `/download`, `/api/agent/*`, `/start/*`, and `/connect.sh` are invalid.
- Command body limit: 64 KB.
- Result body limit: 1 MB.
- Direct signal body limit: 32 KB.
- Direct signal TTL: 60 seconds by default, 120 seconds max.
- Session TTL: 2 hours.
- Expired session cleanup retention: 24 hours.

## Agent Guidance

- First ask the user which machine should run the generated session script.
- Do not invent session IDs; use the code printed by the agent or the `X-Session-Id` header.
- Prefer raw text `/send` bodies for simple commands.
- Use JSON `/send` only for `cwd` or timeout control.
- Keep commands explicit and reversible.
- End sessions with `/end` when finished.
- Do not depend on undocumented response JSON.

## Source

- Repository: `https://github.com/Stoffberg/shell-over-edge`
- README: `https://github.com/Stoffberg/shell-over-edge/blob/main/README.md`
- Agent skill: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Stoffberg/shell-over-edge/main/skills/shell-over-edge/SKILL.md`
